summary: Matched and documented the fighter collision/grab-target routine `func_80078A2C` by moving the relevant `ftcoll` assembly into `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c`, adding a readable C implementation guarded against a `MUST_MATCH` assembly fallback, and expanding collision-related typing in headers. The PR also pulled much of `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s` into C-side inline asm wrappers and introduced/centralized `Hitbox`, `Hurtbox`, `Tangibility`, and `HitElement` declarations so the new grab-check logic could be expressed in terms of fighter hitboxes, hurtboxes, catch elements, grabbable hurtboxes, team checks, and nearest-grab distance. The author noted it depended on #706, resolved part of #595, and could not be fully matched due to float literal/sdata2 issues.
what_changed: Removed nearly all of `asm/melee/ft/ftcoll.s` and added roughly the same code volume to `src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c`, including many inline-asm `ftcoll` functions and the targeted `func_80078A2C` work.
Added a readable non-`MUST_MATCH` C implementation of `func_80078A2C(HSD_GObj*)` described in-source as checking for grabbable targets.
Kept a `MUST_MATCH` assembly version of `func_80078A2C` around the C version, reflecting the PR note that full matching was blocked by float literals.

The C implementation iterates over fighter GObjs from `lbl_804D782C->x20_fighters`, skips invalid/self/team/flagged targets, checks catch hitboxes against grabbable victim hurtboxes with `func_80007ECC`, rejects targets via `func_80084CE4`, calls `func_80076808`, then records the nearest candidate in `x1A58_interactedFighter`, `x1A5C`, a flag bit on `x221B_flag`, and `unk_grab_val`.
Added helper inlines `ftHitGetPtr(Fighter*, u32)` and `ftGrabDist(Fighter*, Fighter*)` to make the grab routine more readable.

Promoted collision types from vague/local declarations to shared headers: `Hitbox`, `Hurtbox`, `Tangibility`, and `HitElement` were placed under `src/melee/lb/forward.h` and `src/melee/lb/lbcollision.h`.
Updated `Fighter` layout in `types.h` to use `Hitbox x914[4]`, `Hitbox x1064_thrownHitbox`, and `Hurtbox x11A0_fighterHurtbox[15]`; changed `x1A68`/`x1A6A` to `u16`; named `0x216C` as `unk_grab_val`.
Moved the `Tangibility` enum out of `src/melee/it/forward.h`, reducing item-header ownership of a collision concept.
Added prototypes for collision, game-mode, item, and player functions needed by the moved/decompiled code.
smart_moves: Used a `MUST_MATCH` assembly fallback for `func_80078A2C` while still landing a readable C version, which preserves exact-build safety when float literal placement prevents a clean match.
Centralized collision forward declarations in `melee/lb/forward.h` instead of continuing to define `Tangibility` in item headers or using ad hoc `struct _Hitbox`/`ftHurt` declarations.

Converted raw fighter offsets used by the grab routine into semi-typed fields such as `x914` hitboxes, `x11A0_fighterHurtbox`, and `unk_grab_val`, making future collision decompilation less offset-heavy.
Introduced `HitElement_Catch` in a shared enum, making the grab/catch hitbox test legible instead of comparing against a magic element value.
Kept uncertain names conservative: many symbols remain `func_...`, `x...`, or `unk_grab_val`, which is safer than overclaiming semantics while still improving local readability.
decomp_lessons: When a function is logically understood but exact matching is blocked by float literal or `sdata2` placement, a useful intermediate state is to gate exact inline assembly under `MUST_MATCH` and keep a readable C implementation for review and future cleanup.

Large collision routines often require header work first: defining `Hitbox`, `Hurtbox`, `Tangibility`, and `HitElement` made `func_80078A2C` expressible in C without excessive casts or local fake structs.
Do not leave shared gameplay concepts in subsystem-specific forward headers. `Tangibility` was removed from `it/forward.h` and moved into an `lb` collision forward header.

For fighter struct work, replacing filler with typed arrays at known offsets can unlock decompilation of loop-heavy code; here the four hitboxes at `0x914` and hurtbox array at `0x11A0` are key to the grab-target scan.
Reviewer/author comments flagged that enum names may still be wrong. Treat `Tangibility` member names and possible sharing with `Hurtbox::tangibility` as provisional until corroborated by more code.
naming_conventions: `Hitbox` and `Hurtbox` are preferred shared type names over old temporary names such as `struct _Hitbox`, `ftHit`, and `ftHurt`.
`HitElement_*` enum members use descriptive suffixes such as `HitElement_Normal`, `HitElement_Fire`, `HitElement_Catch`, `HitElement_Ground`, `HitElement_Disable`, and `HitElement_Leadead`.
`Tangibility` enum members were introduced as `Vulnerable`, `Invincible`, and `Intangible`, but a review comment warned these names may be wrong.
Existing misspelling `tangiblity` appears in the `Hitbox` field and was preserved in the code, likely to avoid unnecessary churn while matching/decomp work continued.
Newly interpreted fighter fields remained conservative where semantics were not fully established, e.g. `unk_grab_val` rather than a stronger final name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved code from a standalone `.s` file into `ftcoll.c` as `asm void` blocks wrapped by `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`, `#pragma push`, `nofralloc`, and `// clang-format off`.
`func_80078A2C` specifically uses `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` for the exact assembly version and `#else` for readable C.
The PR body says the function could not be fully matched due to float literals; an author review comment says it matched except for adding two entries to `sdata2`.
Explicit float constants such as `lbl_804D82E0 = 0.0F`, `lbl_804D82E4 = 500.0F`, and `lbl_804D8310` appear around the moved `ftcoll` code, reinforcing that literal placement was a matching concern.
The C implementation uses small inline helpers to shape control flow and pointer arithmetic around the assembly's hitbox stride and distance-update behavior.

review_feedback.feedback: Author comment: the function is present and matches except that it adds two entries to `sdata2`; it still needs substantial cleanup.

Review/comment feedback warned that `Tangibility` or its element names are probably wrong, but using an enum is better than leaving the field untyped; it may or may not share an enum with `Hurtbox::tangibility`.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `unk_grab_val`, `x1A58_interactedFighter`, or `x1A5C` after the grab/collision code became better understood.
Search for later changes to `Tangibility`, especially whether `Vulnerable`, `Invincible`, and `Intangible` were renamed or split between hitboxes and hurtboxes.
Look for follow-up work resolving the `func_80078A2C` float literal / `sdata2` mismatch so the `MUST_MATCH` assembly fallback could be removed.
Search for later cleanup of `ftcoll.c` inline assembly functions added in this PR, especially `func_80076640` through `func_80078998`.
Inspect later decompilation of `func_80007ECC`, `func_8000ACFC`, and `func_80084CE4` for improved collision type names.
